tier_remaining_note: Tier A remains fully cleared (0). Tier B recomputed from a disk-reconciled rerun of generate_build_plan_v2.py's scoring model on 2026-07-20 (ROUTES_DIR fixed locally to the non-Windows path for this run only, the script file on disk still has the Windows-style path and needs a permanent fix): 433 remaining before chunk 90, minus the 50 Tier B pairs chunk 90 built, leaves 383. Not yet covered by verify_build_state.py's automatic disk reconciliation (only route_pairs/blog/total_markdown_files are); re-run the generator manually each chunk until that gap is closed.
notes: Chunk 90 DONE (Template E, Tier B, 50 new routes, 2 blocks, one batch). Built via 8 parallel research agents, one per country/hub cluster, taking the 50 highest-scored unbuilt Tier B pairs from a disk-reconciled rerun of generate_build_plan_v2.py's scoring model (6,771 built, Tier A=0, Tier B=433 remaining before this chunk). Australia/UAE reciprocal cluster (36 routes): closed the reverse legs for the 18 countries chunk 89 opened one-direction-only (Australia-to-X): Bahamas, Belarus, Bosnia and Herzegovina, Cameroon, Eswatini, Ivory Coast, Kosovo, Kyrgyzstan, Lesotho, Madagascar, Malawi, Moldova, Mongolia, Montenegro, Mozambique, North Macedonia, Senegal, Tajikistan, each now has X-to-Australia and X-to-United-Arab-Emirates. Turkmenistan cluster (3 routes): australia-to-turkmenistan, turkmenistan-to-australia, turkmenistan-to-united-arab-emirates. New Macau hub cluster (6 routes, brand-new pairs never built before): Hong Kong, Japan and South Africa each gained both directions with Macau. UAE reverse-leg cluster (5 routes): united-arab-emirates-to-albania/algeria/angola/bahamas/belarus, completing pairs chunk 89 built export-side only. Genuine differentiators found: DAFF non-approved countries in this batch each picked a source-justified staging country reusing an existing air link rather than a generic detour (France for Cameroon/Ivory Coast, South Africa for Eswatini/Malawi/Mozambique/Lesotho, Seychelles for Madagascar, Singapore for Kyrgyzstan/Tajikistan/Senegal, UK/Poland for Bosnia/Belarus); Montenegro was independently confirmed DAFF Group 3 (not non-approved) and MOCCAE low-risk, a genuine lighter-pathway outlier against its Balkan neighbours. MOCCAE's post-titre wait was cross-checked against USDA APHIS's own UAE page (no fixed wait), overriding several lower-quality aggregator sites that claim a 90-day wait, applied consistently across the batch. Carrier-specific exclusions surfaced fresh this chunk: flydubai carries zero pets on any route despite flying direct into several of these markets; Etihad carries none ex-Johannesburg; Somon Air explicitly refuses animals on its only Dushanbe-Dubai route citing UAE import rules; Air Macau carries no pets at all despite flying Osaka-Macau direct. Macau's new city-pair cluster required fully fresh research since no sibling data existed: Macau is not one of Japan's MAFF six designated regions (full 180-day pathway applies), is not a DALRRD bilateral-agreement country for South Africa, and Hong Kong-Macau moves by ferry/bridge coach rather than air. Two factual inconsistencies were found in already-live sibling pages and deliberately left untouched (out of this chunk's scope, flagged here for a future correction pass): (1) several already-published Australia-bound pages (albania-to-australia.md, botswana-to-australia.md, south-africa-to-united-arab-emirates.md) imply Group 3 countries skip the 180-day post-titre wait and that Perth is a valid PEQ landing point; fresh research on DAFF's own Group 3 guidance confirms the wait still applies and Mickleham (Melbourne) is the only PEQ facility, so this chunk's Group 3 pages (Montenegro) were built on the corrected rule; (2) the existing Kosovo/Kyrgyzstan/Lesotho UK/US sibling pages link to non-existent /pet-transport/origins/ pages for those countries, a pre-existing broken link this chunk's new pages correctly avoid repeating. QA pass after the agents finished caught and trimmed 36 meta descriptions over the 160-character budget in two further passes (the largest single-chunk catch to date), all trimmed while preserving the page-specific differentiator fact; zero em dashes, en dashes or curly quotes across all 50 files; zero banned vocabulary; correct Marcus Webb persona and template_variant E on all 50; valid keyed-map YAML for every requirements block verified with a Python parser cross-checked against the actual route-e-data.html template's named field lookups; route_complexity restricted to the valid low/moderate/high/very_high tokens; zero corpus-wide title/description collisions checked against the full 6,821-file corpus; every sideways/upward link verified to resolve to a real file on disk, zero broken links. A handful of files carry extra route_data keys (country_status, transit_options, staging_country, rnatt, identity_verification, breed_restrictions) beyond the template's rendering whitelist; confirmed harmless (the templates use `with` plus named field access, never a `range` over the whole map, so Hugo silently ignores them) and the underlying facts are already stated in each page's prose sections, but flagged for a future generator-schema tightening pass. Routes 6,771 -> 6,821, pages 7,577 -> 7,627. Chunk 91 (Template A, Tier B) is next.
_previous_notes_chunk_89: Chunk 89 DONE (Template D, Tier B, 50 new routes, 2 blocks, one batch). Built via 7 parallel research agents, one per cluster. Iceland cluster (8 routes): both directions with Hong Kong, Japan, South Africa and South Korea. Canada cluster (14 routes): both directions with Barbados, Belize, Botswana, Guyana, Namibia, Suriname and Zambia. Turkmenistan (1 route): the missing united-states-to-turkmenistan leg. Albania/Algeria/Angola cluster (9 routes): both directions to Australia plus the outbound leg to the UAE for each. Australia export cluster (18 routes, Australia-to-X one direction only): Bahamas, Belarus, Bosnia and Herzegovina, Cameroon, Eswatini, Ivory Coast, Kosovo, Kyrgyzstan, Lesotho, Madagascar, Malawi, Moldova, Mongolia, Montenegro, Mozambique, North Macedonia, Senegal and Tajikistan. Key finds: MAST places Japan in Category 1 but leaves Hong Kong, South Africa and South Korea unclassified; Japan's MAFF treats Iceland as a designated region; CFIA's high-risk list clears Barbados but lists the other six Canada-cluster countries; DAFF confirmed Albania/Algeria/Angola non-approved, each with a source-justified staging country. Routes 6,721 -> 6,771, pages 7,527 -> 7,577. Chunk 90 (Template E, Tier B) was next.
_previous_notes_chunk_88: Chunk 88 DONE (Template C, Tier A + Tier B, 50 new routes, 2 blocks, one batch). Built via 14 parallel research agents. Closed out ALL 12 remaining Tier A pairs (Iceland x UK/US/Australia/UAE/Canada both directions, 10 routes; Macau x UAE both directions, 2 routes). Filled the remaining 38 routes with the highest-scored unbuilt Tier B pairs: UK/US import-side reverse legs for 16 countries (both directions, 32 routes); Turkmenistan's UK leg (1 route); missing US legs for Albania, Algeria, Angola, Bahamas and Belarus (5 routes). Key finds: UK/Iceland/UAE all MAST Category 1; US/Canada MAST Category 2; Iceland DAFF Group 2 for Australia and CFIA rabies-free for Canada; Bosnia and Herzegovina's own Annex IV list places UK in Part A, US in Part B, both titre-exempt. Routes 6,671 -> 6,721, pages 7,477 -> 7,527. Chunk 89 (Template D, Tier B) was next.

counts.note: total_site_pages counts content .md source files. The full deployed total (including Hugo taxonomy and list pages) is verified from sitemap.xml after a build.

counts_method: Reconciled from disk by verify_build_state.py. Never hand-edit count fields; run 'python verify_build_state.py --write' instead.
_dropped_from_rolling_window: Chunks 80, 81, 82-83, 84, 85-86 and 87 notes were dropped to cap file growth (git history still has them in full at commits 4ecac2f1, 3741f752, ef4d776e, ca50c52f, bd68e3c5 and 0cac6bc3). A rolling window of the two most recent previous-chunk notes (currently chunk 89 and chunk 88) is kept going forward.
